Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 09:34 ET
Dispersion hedge dominates as institutions buy index puts while retail chases single-name calls
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Massive P/C divergence reveals institutional dispersion strategy: index puts (1.55 ratio) vs single-name calls (0.88) with $-7.2B negative gamma amplifying moves.
  • 2. NVDA vol structure inverts with 106k Apr puts against 200k+ Jun calls — hedged upside bet into semiconductor earnings season.
  • 3. NFP in 48 hours with negative gamma creates explosive downside risk below SPY $630 put wall.
NARRATIVE — negative gamma dispersion

The most striking signal today is the extreme P/C divergence revealing institutional dispersion positioning ahead of Friday's jobs data. Index ETFs show heavy put buying at 1.55 ratio while single-name stocks see call-heavy flow at 0.88 — a classic hedge fund structure betting on stock-picking alpha while hedging systematic risk. The $-7.2B negative gamma in SPY amplifies this setup, with dealers forced to sell into weakness and buy into strength around the critical $630 put wall where 268k contracts cluster. NVDA's complex flow structure tells the semiconductor story in options terms. The 106k April $160 puts represent near-term downside hedging, while massive June call buying (200k+ contracts in $140/$150 strikes) positions for post-earnings recovery. This matches the technical death cross signal but maintains longer-term bullish conviction — exactly the structure you'd expect with implied correlation at 0.31 enabling single-name dispersion trades. The rich vol/call heavy divergence in names like CRM and MRK suggests similar hedged-optimism positioning across growth sectors. Critical risk lies in Friday's NFP catalyst intersecting with current negative gamma. The combination of oversold technicals (SPY RSI 27), elevated VIX at 28, and dealer short positioning creates conditions for violent moves. Below $630, put gamma accelerates selling pressure toward the $620 zone where technical support converges with gamma flip levels.

Layer 1: Sentiment -29Layer 2: GEX -$7.2BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.55/0.88Layer 9: NFP 2d
NVDAVOL PLAY
Hedged upside structure
Semiconductor earnings approach drives complex NVDA positioning with 106k April $160 puts hedging 200k+ June calls in $140/$150 strikes. Death cross technical signal conflicts with longer-term bullish options positioning, creating classic risk-managed growth bet into earnings season.
L3: 106k put blockL3: 200k call sweepL5: death crossL4: backwardation

SPYBEARISH
Put wall acceleration
Massive put clustering at $630 strike with 268k open interest creates gamma acceleration zone. Negative $7.2B dealer gamma means breaks below this level trigger forced selling into oversold RSI 27 conditions. NFP catalyst in 48 hours adds explosive risk.
L2: -$7.2B gammaL3: 268k puts $630L9: NFP 2dL5: RSI oversold
Short SPY below $630, target $620 gamma flip
XLEBULLISH
Energy momentum breakout
Energy sector shows strongest technical score at 86.7 with golden cross and overbought RSI 77, contrasting sharply with broader market weakness. MACD positive momentum continues as oil vol climbs, creating sector rotation opportunity while indices struggle.

ATM IV = at-the-money implied vol  |  IV PCTILE = where today's IV ranks vs ≈90 trading days (green=cheap, red=expensive)  |  Spread = ATM IV − RV 20d  |  Rich: IV/RV >1.2×  |  Skew 90/110: P(90%) − C(110%)  |  SKEW PCTILE: vs ≈90d history  |  PUT/CALL V%: daily volume rank vs ≈90d history  |  vs SECTOR: ticker ATM IV / sector ETF ATM IV (±10% threshold)  |  Divergence: cross-signal flags (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT, CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW, etc.)
